previousFix.description: Designed options + picked the best myself, as asked. Before: a bare flat list (Strength - best X kg x Y - Nd ago) floating between the carded sections. After: a Personal bests CARD that matches the activity-page card rhythm - a calm header with an orange N

previousFix.description: 2nd-pass redesign per your note (design in cards, nice badass UI interactive and dynamic). The PR section is now a swipeable horizontal CAROUSEL of PR cards: each lift is its own card with the weight as a big hero number (140 kg, 120 kg...), an orange dumbbell domain accent + trophy header, snap-scroll (swipe for more), and a staggered entrance animation. Bold + dynamic, still on-brand (no XP bars / tier medals - calm-gamification guardrails). Same /strength/summary data. Frontend StrengthStrip.tsx (421c82dd); verified live on the activity page with seeded PRs (before/after attached). I picked this direction myself as asked. Visible in the app after the next release.

history.note: when you click on them it should be like pokemon cards and attached videos etc nice full screen ui

previousFix.description: Ran it live on /activity (your reply: 'run it and give screenshot'). The flat repeated 'best 20kg x10' PR list is now the swipeable 'Personal bests' carousel: trophy header + '6 this month' pill, one card per lift with the weight as the hero (Conventional Deadlift 140kg, Barbell Back Squat 120kg, ...), reps + how-long-ago underneath, third card peeking to signal horizontal swipe. Sits cleanly above Today's Workout / Pull Day A. Calm/tier-free per the design guardrails. Screenshot attached is the actual running build (commit 421c82dd).

history.note: add some badges for the pr and breaking own PR's and automaticalilly add to leaderboard and leaderboard standings shown here also the full screen UI missing when clicked on cards.

previousFix.description: Built the full-screen collectable card. Tap any Personal-best card → it opens like a trading card (screenshot): orange gradient art zone with a TROPHY (top-left) + strength emblem + a slow holographic foil shimmer, over a clean stat panel — big weight hero (140kg), est. 1RM chip (~163kg), reps + date. Flip-in animation, tap-outside / X / Esc to close, reduced-motion safe. This is the 'pokemon cards / nice full screen ui + trophies' part. The 'attached videos' half is reserved by the 'Add a clip' button — that's a separate next step (it needs video upload + storage + playback; quick Q when you're ready: should the clip be the user recording/uploading their own PR lift, stored on our backend? I'll build it that way unless you'd rather link an external URL). Frontend 1de68415, visible after the next app release.

issues.reviewReason: Good news on the badges: the backend ALREADY has a PR badge system — badges for your 1st PR, 5 / 25 / 100 PRs, AND breaking your own PR is already detected (it logs the improvement + awards XP + a feed event). So that engine exists; what's missing is (1) SURFACING those badges on the Personal-bests section/cards so you can SEE them, and (2) the LEADERBOARD, which is a brand-new feature. Quick product call before I build the leaderboard (so I build the right one): a PR leaderboard ranks people by WHAT and among WHOM? My recommendation — a 'Strength' tab on the existing leaderboard that ranks your FRIENDS by total PRs (tie-break by estimated total 1RM), with your standing highlighted; auto-updates when you log a PR. Reply with that or your preferred metric/scope (e.g. global vs friends, single-lift vs total) and I'll build it. I'll surface the earned PR badges on the PR section in the meantime. (The collectable card itself is in review separately.)

issues.description: When the build activity plan open the activity page directly with prefilled current workout in the entire page

fix.description: Re-fixed per 'we should see the bottom ui with our existing plan'. The problem was the builder opened EMPTY ('Day 1 of 7 - 0 built'), ignoring your current plan (my earlier targets-only sheet was the wrong call - reverted). Now Build Activity Plan opens PREFILLED with your existing plan: the workout section (bottom UI) shows your current exercises - screenshot shows 'Day 1 of 6 - 6 built' with Chin-Up / Bent-Over Row / Upright Row / Preacher Curl / etc. - AND your saved daily-activity targets up top (11,500 steps - 4x cardio), all editable. Save updates that plan (no build-from-scratch). New users with no plan still get a fresh seed. App commit 578ea51d. Visible after the next app release.

history.note: we should see the bottom ui with our existing plan, PLAN THIS AND FIX

previousFix.description: Built it per your note ('don't build the plan again but have the existing plan for exercise'). 'Build Activity Plan' no longer dumps you into the full 7-day workout builder. It now opens a focused Daily Activity editor (shot 1) PREFILLED from your existing plan (here 11,500 steps, 4x cardio) — tap to adjust steps/cardio/NEAT, then 'Save & view activity' saves ONLY the targets and drops you on the Activity page (shot 2: Steps 0/11,500). Your workout stays exactly as it was (still '0 of 6 exercises' — no rebuild). New backend PUT /onboarding/custom-plan/activity-targets upserts the targets onto your active plan; nothing touches the workout days. Backend 4793d3e + frontend 7b916b05, both deployed/pushed; verified end-to-end (target round-trips and the plan's 6 workout days are untouched). Visible after the next app release.

issues.reviewReply: yes build it and make sure we don't build the plan again but have the existing plan for exercise

issues.description: Calories overshoot then orignal proposed plan
issues.reviewReason: Dug into this with prod data. The diet plan has no stored 'target' — the kcal shown (2991) is the SUM of the assembled meals. On the test account the pipeline is CORRECT: computed target 1824 == assembled meals 1824 (TDEE 2189 minus deficit, floored at BMR), so I can't reproduce a 2991-style overshoot there. Most likely cause: the build wizard's PROPOSED target comes from the client calc (calcOnboardingStats) which has a goal-completion-DATE trajectory override (a steeper deficit to hit your target date), but the SAVED/displayed plan uses the server calc (calculate_target_calories) which is deficit-only with NO date logic. If a date was set, the proposed (lower) and the saved (higher) legitimately diverge -> looks like an overshoot. The builder also tolerates up to +10% over target before blocking. To fix the RIGHT one safely (this calc drives everyone's calorie goals) I need: the affected account's email, OR did you set a goal completion date on that build, OR the proposed number you saw vs the 2991? Once I know which target should win (date-driven trajectory or standard deficit), I'll reconcile the two calcs so the proposed and the saved plan always match.
issues.reviewReply: it was different during the onboarding
